english: the ground, sá-la (sa la) (W. also *se kú-na* (se ku na)) ₒdúg-pa ('dug pa) to sit on the ground, sá-la ltuṅ-ba (sa la ltung ba), gyél-ba (gyel ba) to fall to the ground; sa-ₒóg (sa 'og), sa-stéṅ (sa steng), sa-blá (sa bla), under, on, above the ground; sa-ₒog-spyód (sa 'og spyod) the Nagas (klu (klu)); the earth, the globe which we inhabit, usually more accurately sa čén-po (sa chen po) v. above. - 2. place, spot, space, = gnas (gnas), and col. more in use than this, rwai sa ₒbúr-ba (rwa'i sa 'bur ba) Stg. 'swollen in the places of the horns', i.e. men that had been oxen in a former life, and in consequence of it are distinguished by little knobs corresponding to their former horns; *póg-sa ghá-la dug* (pog sa gha la dug) C. where have you been hit or hurt? ₒdá-sa ('da' sa) and ₒbém-sa ('bem sa) sharp-shooters' stand and place of the target C.; yod-sa (yod sa) the place where a person lives, (in the old classical style usually expressed by gaṅ-na-ba (gang na ba)); *čiṅ-taṅ-sa* (cing tang sa) vulg. 1. orifice of the urethra. 2. privy, water-closet; ṅá-la grós-ₒdri-sa čig yod (nga la gros 'dri sa cig yod) I have a place where to ask advice, I have an oracle Glr.; in a wider sense: occasion, opportunity, possibility, lús-la rég-sa med (lus la reg sa med) one cannot get near him Glr., rje kyód-kyi gśégs-sa dé-na med (rje khyod kyi gshegs sa de na med) you cannot go to that place, Sir! Mil.; nor-gyis blú-sa med (nor gyis blu sa med) you cannot ransom yourself by money Mil.; also with respect to men: ṅa gzán-la zér-sa (nga gzan la zer sa) (zú-sa (zu sa), ré-sa (re sa)) med (med) I cannot address myself to any body else with my words (requests, hopes); place, step, degree, grade, čuṅ-ma čé-sar bzuṅ (chung ma che sar bzung) he took and treated his second wife in the place of the first, i.e. he showed the second the honour due to the first; sa-bču (sa bcu) v. compounds. - 3. it is also said to be the name of a quadruped of the size and appearance of a badger, but not identic with gsa (gsa) Sik. Comp. sa-dkár (sa dkar) = dkar-rtsi (dkar rtsi) Cs. -- sa-skám (sa skam) Sch. arid soil, dry ground, steppe. -- sa-skyóṅ (sa skyong), sa-skyór (sa skyor) Lex. protector of the earth i.e. king. -- sa-ku (sa ku) made dirty by earth, dust etc., soiled, turbid. -- *sa-kyád* (sa khyad) W. (for gnas (gnas) B., sá-ču (sa chu) C.) place, *sa-kyád kám-po* (sa khyad kam po) a dry place, also: the dry land; a piece of ground, *sa-kyád čig taṅ* (sa khyad cig tang) he gave him landed property, *sa-kyád-di dág-po* (sa khyad di dag po) landlord; dwelling-place, place of residence, *kyód-di sa-kyád gá-ru yod* (khyod di sa khyad ga ru yod) where is your home? -- sa-kyáb (sa kyab) Cs. = sa-bdag (sa bdag). -- sa-kyoṅ (sa khyong) Cs.: 'the earth's extension or compass'. -- *sa-ṭa* (sa ta) (sa-kru (sa khru)) map C. -- sá-mkʻan (sa mkhan) one who is well acquainted with a particular place or country, a guide Dzl. -- sa-mkʻar (sa mkhar) Glr. a castle the walls of which consist for the most part of earth. -- sa-gyóṅ (sa gyong) Sch. hard ground. -- sa-dgá (sa dga') and sa-dgyés (sa dgyes) Lex. = ku-mu-da (ku mu da). -- sa-dgra (sa dgra) Glr. the enemy of a country, i.e. in many cases nothing but a demon. -- *saṅ-gúl* (sang gul) W. earthquake. -- sa-ṅós (sa ngos) surface of the earth. -- sa-sṅóṅ (sa sngon) Cs. blue earth. -- sa-bču (sa bcu), दशभूमि, acc. to one explanation the ten steps or degrees of perfection which must be attained by those striving after the prize of Buddhaship; sa tʻób-pa (sa thob pa) to reach one step (viz. the first) Do.; sa čén-po (sa chen po) a high degree, e.g. the eighth Thgy.; Foucaux enumerates them all Gyatch. Transl. p. 3. According to another supposition sa-bču (sa bcu) signifies the ten worlds or dominions of the Bodhisattvas Was. (124). -- sa-čú (sa chu) Glr., Mil., C. place, country (W. *sa-kyád* (sa khyad)). -- sa-čén (sa chen) 1. v. above sa (sa) 1.; 2. v. sa-bču (sa bcu); 3. v. sa-skya (sa skya). -- sa-stéṅ (sa steng) v. above sa (sa) 1. -- sa-dúg (sa dug) evaporation, damp, injurious to those sleeping on the bare ground. -- sa-dó (sa do) (v. do-po (do po)) half a load of earth, a sackful of earth, being half the load of a donkey Mil. -- *sa-dóṅ* (sa dong) W. pit, hole. -- sa-bdág (sa bdag) 1. landlord, master or lord of the ground, sovereign Stg., sá-yi bdág-po sá-yi rje (sa yi bdag po sa yi rje) are words used in addressing a king Mil. 2. more frq.: god of the ground of the country, supposed to be a jealous and angry being, of terrific appearance, to whom on many occasions sacrifices are brought, and who prob. was worshipped already before the spread of Buddhism cf. Schl. 271. -- sa-mda (sa mda') 1. mouse-trap, also a large trap for catching leopards and other animals. 2. a fabulous plant (?). -- sa-rdó (sa rdo) a stone of earthy fracture; earth and stones; *sa-dó ḍa tsí-wa* (sa do da tsi wa) to slight, to disregard, to neglect C. -- sa-gnás (sa gnas) (= gnas-sa (gnas sa)) place, region, country, landscape, nyams-dgá-bai sa-gnás (nyams dga' ba'i sa gnas) a lovely landscape Glr. -- sa-sna-lṅá (sa sna lnga) soil of five different places. -- sá- (sa-)
